(c) which related to quality assurance regarding screening for breast cancer, (d) which related to quality assurance regarding screening for cervical cancer, and (e) which related to issuance by Secretary of guidelines with respect to quality of mammography and cytological services.Pub. L. 103–183

Transition Rule Regarding Mammographies, , , provided that:Pub. L. 103–183, title I, § 101(c)(2)107 Stat. 2228Dec. 14, 1993“With respect to the screening procedure for breast cancer known as a mammography, the requirements in effect on the day before the date of the enactment of this Act [] under section 1503(c) of the Public Health Service Act [] remain in effect (for an individual or facility conducting such procedures pursuant to a grant to a State under section 1501 of such Act []) until there is in effect for the facility a certificate (or provisional certificate) issued under section 354 of such Act [].”Dec. 14, 199342 U.S.C. 300m(c)42 U.S.C. 300k42 U.S.C. 263b

children.heading: Requirement of provision of all services by date certain
children.label: a Requirement of provision of all services by date certain

children.text: to ensure that, initially and throughout the period during which amounts are received pursuant to the grant, not less than 60 percent of the grant is expended to provide each of the services or activities described in paragraphs (1) and (2) of , including making available screening procedures for both breast and cervical cancers;section 300k(a) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of breast cancer, both a physical examination of the breasts and the screening procedure known as a mammography are conducted; and

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of cervical cancer, both a pelvic examination and the screening procedure known as a pap smear are conducted;
to ensure that, by the end of any second fiscal year of payments pursuant to the grant, each of the services or activities described in is provided; andsection 300k(a) of this title
to ensure that not more than 40 percent of the grant is expended to provide the services or activities described in paragraphs (3) through (6) of such section.

children.heading: Use of improved screening procedures
children.label: b Use of improved screening procedures
children.text: The Secretary may not make a grant under unless the State involved agrees that, if any screening procedure superior to a procedure described in subsection (a)(2) becomes commonly available and is recommended for use, any entity providing screening procedures pursuant to the grant will utilize the superior procedure rather than the procedure described in such subsection.section 300k of this title

children.heading: Quality assurance regarding screening procedures
children.label: c Quality assurance regarding screening procedures
children.text: The Secretary may not make a grant under unless the State involved agrees that the State will, in accordance with applicable law, assure the quality of screening procedures conducted pursuant to such section.section 300k of this title

children.heading: Waiver of services requirement on division of funds
children.label: d Waiver of services requirement on division of funds
A
children.text: the State involved will use the waiver to leverage non-Federal funds to supplement each of the services or activities described in paragraphs (1) and (2) of ;section 300k(a) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: the application of such requirement would result in a barrier to the enrollment of qualifying women;

children.label: i
children.text: demonstrates, to the satisfaction of the Secretary, the manner in which the State will use such waiver to expand the level of screening and follow-up services provided immediately prior to the date on which the waiver is granted; and

children.label: ii
children.text: provides assurances, satisfactory to the Secretary, that the State will, on an annual basis, demonstrate, through such documentation as the Secretary may require, that the State has used such waiver as described in clause (i);

children.label: I
children.text: the level of the State fiscal year expenditures for such services and activities for the fiscal year preceding the first fiscal year for which the waiver is granted; or

children.label: II
children.text: at the option of the State and upon approval by the Secretary, the average level of the State expenditures for such services and activities for the 3-fiscal year period preceding the first fiscal year for which the waiver is granted; and

children.label: ii
children.text: a plan, satisfactory to the Secretary, for maintaining the level of activities carried out under the waiver after the expiration of the waiver and any extension of such waiver;

children.label: E
children.text: the Secretary finds that granting such a waiver to a State will increase the number of women in the State that receive each of the services or activities described in paragraphs (1) and (2) of , including making available screening procedures for both breast and cervical cancers; andsection 300k(a) of this title

children.label: F
children.text: the Secretary finds that granting such a waiver to a State will not adversely affect the quality of each of the services or activities described in paragraphs (1) and (2) of .section 300k(a) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: shall grant such waivers for a period that is not less than 1 year but not more than 2 years; and

children.label: ii
children.text: upon request of a State, may extend a waiver for an additional period that is not less than 1 year but not more than 2 years in accordance with subparagraph (B).

children.label: i
children.text: without an extension of the waiver, there will be a barrier to the enrollment of qualifying women;

children.label: ii
children.text: the State requesting such extended waiver will use the waiver to leverage non-Federal funds to supplement the services or activities described in paragraphs (1) and (2) of ;section 300k(a) of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: the waiver has increased, and will continue to increase, the number of women in the State that receive the services or activities described in paragraphs (1) and (2) of ;section 300k(a) of this title

children.label: iv
children.text: the waiver has not, and will not, result in lower quality in the State of the services or activities described in paragraphs (1) and (2) of ; andsection 300k(a) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: the level of the State fiscal year expenditures for such services and activities for the fiscal year preceding the first fiscal year for which the waiver is granted; or

children.label: II
children.text: at the option of the State and upon approval by the Secretary, the average level of the State expenditures for such services and activities for the 3-fiscal year period preceding the first fiscal year for which the waiver is granted.

children.label: A
children.text: A description of the total amount of dollars leveraged annually from Non-Federal entities in States receiving a waiver under paragraph (1) and how these amounts were used.2So in original. Probably should be “non-Federal”.2

children.label: i
children.text: paragraphs (1) and (2) of ; andsection 300k(a) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: paragraphs (3) through (6) of .section 300k(a) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: A description of the number of States receiving waivers under paragraph (1) annually.

children.label: i
children.text: the number of women receiving services under paragraphs (1), (2), and (3) of in programs before and after the granting of such waiver; andsection 300k(a) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: the average annual level of State fiscal expenditures for the services and activities described in paragraphs (1) and (2) of for the year preceding the first year for which the waiver was granted.section 300k(a) of this title

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 4 Limitation
children.text: Amounts to which a waiver applies under this subsection shall not be used to increase the number of salaried employees.

children.heading: Indian tribe
children.label: A Indian tribe
children.text: The term “Indian tribe” has the meaning given the term in .section 1603 of title 25

children.heading: Tribal organization
children.label: B Tribal organization
children.text: The term “tribal organization” has the meaning given the term in .section 1603 of title 25

children.heading: State
children.label: C State
children.text: The term “State” means each of the several States of the United States, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, American Samoa, the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands, the Republic of the Marshall Islands, the Federated States of Micronesia, the Republic of Palau, an Indian tribe, and a tribal organization.

children.heading: Sunset
children.label: 6 Sunset
children.text: The Secretary may not grant a waiver or extension under this subsection after .September 30, 2012
